text: Public speaking is one of the most common fears (glossophobia) — yet also one of the most valuable skills. Key principles: know your audience, structure (intro-body-conclusion), practice aloud, use stories, control pace, make eye contact. It's not about being perfect — it's about connecting. Toastmasters: global public speaking practice organizatio
Opening: hook (story, question, statistic) in first 30 seconds.
Slides: less text, more visuals, 10/20/30 rule (Kawasaki: 10 slides, 20 minutes, 30pt font).
